name: North Karelia (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the gules field of the historic Karelian arms
the silver charges of the arms
the golden crowns of the arms

description: A rectangular red flag charged centrally with the silver-and-gold provincial arms of Karelia: two crowned arms grasping a Western sword and an Eastern scimitar.
history: North Karelia (Pohjois-Karjala) is one of the regions formed in Finland's 1997 administrative reform. Its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, drawn from the historic provincial arms of Karelia.

features.symbolism: two crowned arms grasping a sword and a scimitar that meet point-to-point on the red field, derived from the historic provincial arms of Karelia and symbolising the long contest between the Western and Eastern Christian spheres over the region.
